target.title: fix(doctor): report managed plugin version drift
target.body:
﻿## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- `openclaw doctor` now surfaces active official managed plugin version drift that was previously only visible in `openclaw gateway status --deep`.
- Doctor uses daemon-context status data so the warning is based on the running local gateway version, not the caller CLI version.
- The drift probe is local-gateway only, skips misleading remote-mode local install-record checks, and avoids executing daemon-side exec SecretRefs unless `--allow-exec` is set.

Why does this matter now?

- Closes #90891, where a live stable core upgrade left the official managed `codex` plugin on `2026.5.30-beta.1` while the gateway was `2026.6.1`, and doctor missed the mismatch.

What is the intended outcome?

- Doctor emits a `Plugin version drift` note with the stale plugin version, expected gateway version, and an actionable `openclaw plugins update <plugin> && openclaw gateway restart` repair.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Hook drift, non-official plugin version policy, and changing the existing gateway-status drift detector.

What does success look like?

- A local managed official plugin drift appears in doctor output, while remote gateway mode and auth-skipped probes do not produce misleading local drift advice.

What should reviewers focus on?

- `runWorkspaceStatusHealth` now obtains drift from `gatherDaemonStatus`, plus the exec SecretRef guard in `status.gather.ts` and the local-probe credential policy reuse.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #90891

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #90917 was an overlapping contributor PR; it has now been closed. This PR carries the proofed fix.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Yes. The issue reporter/maintainer account `brokemac79` owns the affected VPS proof environment and asked for this focused fix.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw doctor --non-interactive` reports official managed plugin version drift for a stale active `codex` install.
- Real environment tested: `myvps` (`polymarket-mc`), Ubuntu Linux, fresh temp clone of this PR branch at head `8ac8873f6952b4855c6b677e8906ce424cf218b4`, local gateway running `2026.6.1`, source checkout CLI `2026.6.2`.
- Exact setup verified before proof:
  ```bash
  cd /tmp/oc-90891-latest
  git rev-parse HEAD
  # 8ac8873f6952b4855c6b677e8906ce424cf218b4
  COREPACK_ENABLE_DOWNLOAD_PROMPT=0 corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
  sed -n '1,220p' /tmp/openclaw-90891-doctor-proof-current/config.json
  sed -n '1,80p' /tmp/openclaw-90891-doctor-proof-current/state/npm/node_modules/@openclaw/codex/openclaw.plugin.json
  ```
- Exact command run after this patch:
  ```bash
  cd /tmp/oc-90891-latest
  env OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-90891-doctor-proof-current/state \
      OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-90891-doctor-proof-current/config.json \
      COREPACK_ENABLE_DOWNLOAD_PROMPT=0 \
      corepack pnpm openclaw doctor --non-interactive \
      | tee /tmp/openclaw-90891-doctor-proof-latest.out
  grep -F 'Plugin version drift' /tmp/openclaw-90891-doctor-proof-latest.out
  grep -F 'codex: 2026.5.30-beta.1 (npm) -> expected' /tmp/openclaw-90891-doctor-proof-latest.out
  grep -F 'openclaw plugins update codex && openclaw gateway restart' /tmp/openclaw-90891-doctor-proof-latest.out
  ```
- Evidence after fix on head `8ac8873f6952`:
  ```text
  ◇  Plugin version drift ────────────────────────────────────────────╮
  │                                                                   │
  │  1 active official plugin not on OpenClaw 2026.6.1                │
  │  - codex: 2026.5.30-beta.1 (npm) -> expected 2026.6.1             │
  │  Fix: openclaw plugins update codex && openclaw gateway restart.  │
  │                                                                   │
  ├───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
  ```
- Observed result after fix: doctor displayed the drift note using the running gateway version (`2026.6.1`) and an exact single-plugin repair command.
- What was not tested: full production plugin downgrade/upgrade on the live persistent `~/.openclaw` state; proof used an isolated temp config/state with a managed npm fixture and the real running local gateway.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Mantis tooling was not available. Crabbox was attempted earlier but not usable: Blacksmith Testbox was unauthenticated/repo resolution failed, and Azure Crabbox allocation `cbx_2113c24a2c6f` / `run_e119becc434e` could not sync because local Windows `rsync.exe` was missing behind the shim.
- Before evidence: issue #90891 includes the affected host output where `gateway status --deep` reported `codex: 2026.5.30-beta.1 (npm) -> expected 2026.6.1`, while doctor only showed plugin counts/errors.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```bash
# Local Windows focused validation
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/commands/doctor-workspace-status.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts src/gateway/probe-auth.test.ts
node --import tsx scripts/check-no-extension-test-core-imports.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.camera.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.live.config.ts extensions/google/google.live.test.ts extensions/minimax/minimax.live.test.ts
git diff --check
codex review --base origin/main

# Local limitation
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts -t "passes SIGTERM abort signals into local agent runs"
# Blocked locally on Windows by pre-existing temp cleanup EPERM in withTempStore before the selected test body ran.

# VPS latest-head real behavior proof
cd /tmp/oc-90891-latest
git rev-parse HEAD
COREPACK_ENABLE_DOWNLOAD_PROMPT=0 corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
env OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-90891-doctor-proof-current/state \
    OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-90891-doctor-proof-current/config.json \
    COREPACK_ENABLE_DOWNLOAD_PROMPT=0 \
    corepack pnpm openclaw doctor --non-interactive
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Doctor workspace status formatter renders supplied drift reports and exact single-plugin repair advice.
- Doctor flow passes daemon-context drift when a confirmed daemon/gateway drift report is available and probe auth was not skipped.
- Doctor skips the drift status probe for remote gateway mode.
- Status gather disables exec SecretRef materialization by default while preserving normal local probe credential policy, including ignoring remote exec SecretRefs for local probes.
- Agent CLI SIGTERM tests now wait for the signal bridge rather than racing embedded-agent import/migration startup.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- Doctor did not show any plugin version drift note for the reported stale official managed plugin state.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Doctor can now emit a new `Plugin version drift` warning.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes, narrowly. The new doctor drift probe can read daemon-context gateway auth, but exec SecretRefs are disabled by default and require `--allow-exec`, matching doctor's existing exec gate.

What is the highest-risk area?

Avoiding misleading local install-record drift warnings and avoiding unexpected exec SecretRef execution.

How is that risk mitigated?

The probe is skipped for remote gateway mode, drift is suppressed when auth materialization was skipped, and tests cover the exec SecretRef and remote-mode boundaries. Local probe credential gating reuses the same remote-auth stripping policy as Gateway probes.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

No author-side code/proof work is pending. CI is green on head `8ac8873f6952b4855c6b677e8906ce424cf218b4`: 135 success, 20 intentionally skipped, 1 neutral CodeQL summary, no failures. `checks-node-agentic-commands-agent-channel` passed on the latest CI run after the test synchronization fix.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Codex review findings were addressed locally before PR creation and during CI cleanup:
- Use running gateway context instead of CLI-only workspace status.
- Do not execute daemon-side exec SecretRefs before doctor's `--allow-exec` gate.
- Match local probe credential policy so remote SecretRefs do not block local probes.
- Skip drift probes for remote doctor runs.
- Keep daemon fallback drift available when no auth warning is present, with latest-head VPS proof showing the running gateway version is used for the reported upgrade case.
- Stabilize the local-agent SIGTERM test by synchronizing on signal listener registration.

Codex review final result on head `8ac8873f6952`: `codex review --base origin/main` reported no discrete correctness issues.
target.author: brokemac79
